body_txt: Usually featured in comedy works, this is any very, very, very long limousine belonging to some rich showoff jerkass who may or may not be Compensating for Something . Often used as a (no pun intended) Overly Long Gag when a good portion of a scene is reserved for watching a limo take a lifetime to pull over. Of course, the main character will land inside (usually more or less forcefully) at least once. Sometimes the absurdly long vehicle will have nonsensical features like a hot-tub, bar, or a bowling alley attached, or perhaps a small car inside the limousine to help people travel within it more quickly. Also expect shenanigans if someone wants to stand up in the roof. Someone coming out from this car will usually use a Feet-First Introduction . And don't try to ask whether this thing is street legal or how it can make turns at corners (although in a wacky cartoon, it may bend to do so); you would be missing the joke . A cousin of the Pimped-Out Car , more specifically the Rice Burner and Hummer Dinger . They're all about cars modified that make their owners look worthy of mockery, though in different ways: Absurdly Long Limousines rely on exaggerated lengths, while Rice Burners rely on exaggerated styling and Hummer Dingers on exaggerated manliness. See also Sunroof Shenanigans .
